AI really has no limits 😭

Somehow it managed to create a Lord of the Rings x Pawn Shop crossover where Frodo is literally just standing in a pawn shop trying to sell the One Ring. No epic quest, no Mount Doom, no dramatic music — just Frodo holding the most powerful ring in Middle-earth like it's some random item he brought in to trade.

It's one of those things that makes absolutely no sense but at the same time feels weirdly perfect. AI just casually mixing two completely different worlds and somehow making it look normal.

We've officially reached the timeline where the One Ring ends up at a pawn shop.

Credit: @thedungerzone

AI is turning internet mashups into cinematic scenes.
Part 2 of "Wrong Universe" shows how generative video tools can blend completely different worlds - mixing The Lord of the Rings with Pawn Stars.

What used to require actors, sets, and production crews can now be created with AI video generation and a few prompts.

Scenes like this - featuring Frodo, Rick Harrison, and even the Witch-King - are fully AI-generated experiments in storytelling, where creators collide universes that could never meet on screen.

The technology behind generative video is evolving fast, turning memes and ideas into mini movie scenes in minutes.

AI has done wonders 🤖✨
With artificial intelligence, you can think of anything… and somehow it actually does it 😂
From smart ideas to funny moments this is the future, and it's hilarious!

Who else is amazed (and laughing) at what AI can do? 😄

A creator used AI to produce "Harry Potter by Balenciaga (2026)", a cinematic parody that looks closer to a short film than a meme.

Just a few years ago, in 2023, most AI videos could barely animate subtle head movements or blinking.

Now creators can generate entire scenes, characters, and camera motion with tools like Seedance and other generative video models.

The jump from simple clips to almost full movie scenes happened in less than three years.

AI video is quickly turning the internet into a new kind of film studio.

If you remember the original 2023 "Harry Potter by Balenciaga" videos…
you're officially an AI generation OG.

Comment CHARACTER for this New AI Video Generator.

With the new AI tool, Character 3, creating videos is easier than ever. Imagine this: you want to make a video for your small business. You don't have the budget to hire an influencer. What do you do? Enter Character 3.

This tool lets you create hyper-realistic talking videos. Think perfect lip sync, natural expressions, and full-body movement. It's like having your own actor without the cost.

How does it work? It's simple. Character 3 is the first AI omni model. It processes text, images, and audio all at once. This means you can create videos that look and sound real.

Why is this important? Small businesses can now compete with bigger brands. You can produce user-generated content (UGC) without breaking the bank.

Here's what you can do with Character 3:
* Make marketing videos
* Create engaging social media content
* Produce explainer videos for your products

I tried it myself. I entered some text and uploaded a few images. Within minutes, I had a video that looked amazing. It felt like magic.

So, if you're looking to boost your brand's presence, give Character 3 a shot. It might just be what you need to stand out in a crowded market.

CEO Warning on AI Competition

Sundar Pichai warned employees that 2026 will be an "intense" year marked by fierce AI competition and mounting cloud computing demand. He cautioned staff: "You can't rest on your laurels. We have a lot of hard work ahead". Pichai also acknowledged "elements of irrationality" in the AI investment boom and warned that "no company is going to be immune" if an AI bubble were to burst.

Harry Potter gets a completely new look through AI, with a bold drippy style that transforms the familiar world into something fresh and visually striking. It feels less like a remake and more like an entirely different universe shaped by pure imagination.

What makes it even more impressive is how simple the process has become. A single prompt can generate a detailed cinematic scene in seconds, turning an idea into a full visual without the need for complex tools or skills at all.

There are no actors, no crew, and no equipment involved in the process at all. It is just AI taking a single thought and turning it into reality, showing how powerful and accessible modern creativity has truly become for anyone with an idea.

Harry Potter fans are reimagining Snape using AI - and it's dividing opinions.

The character is instantly recognizable, yet completely different. A familiar identity, reshaped through new tools.

Some see it as creative exploration. Others see it as crossing a line.

But that tension is the point.

As AI makes it easier to reinterpret iconic characters, the question shifts from can this be done to should it be done.

The tech billionaire predicted that AI would replace humans for most things, but that doesn't make us powerless.

Ultimately, it's up to us to decide how we use AI and which parts of the work we want to keep doing ourselves, Gates explained.

With that in mind, the philanthropist named three professions he believes are safe from AI takeover.

These are coders, biologists and energy experts.

Furthermore, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman announced AI would eventually replace software engineers, and so, he suggests that students should 'master AI tools' like his generation did with coding.

But Gates believes while AI can now generate code and even pass technical benchmarks, human coders are still essential to the process.

Human professionals will be needed to identify and correct errors, refine algorithms and support AI's development, Gates described.
